title: Finite Divided Difference Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Newton divided-difference bad interpolation value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational Newton divided-difference replay for f(x)=1+x^2 at nodes 0,1,2 computes interpolated_value = 10 at x=3, while a malformed row claims interpolated_value = 9, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional interpolation rows only when they add distinct node-ordering, barycentric, spline, conditioning, floating-point, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep general interpolation error and convergence theory in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed polynomial, nodes, sample values, divided-difference table, Newton coefficients, basis products, terms, and interpolation value as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false interpolation value through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact value.
Expose the pack through polynomial, calculus, real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep general interpolation uniqueness, error estimates, node-choice conditioning, spline theory, floating-point interpolation correctness, and numerical-stability claims marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
